lookup_context: Wake interval examples include super, heavy, and B757 cases
items.task: Use the adjacent-airport figures to choose the applicable wake interval.
items.instruction: Use the adjacent airport operation figures.
items.question_text: For adjacent airport operation, what interval applies to a heavy, large, or small aircraft behind a super aircraft?

choices.text: No wake interval applies between adjacent airports.

items.explanation: The adjacent-airport operation material lists heavy, large, or small behind super as a 3-minute interval.

lookup_context: Operation: arrival landing behind an arriving aircraft requiring wake turbulence separation
items.task: Identify the figure-supported context for applying arrival wake minima.
items.question_text: FIG 6-1-3 supports which nonradar arrival-minima situation?

choices.text: An arrival landing behind an arriving aircraft requiring wake turbulence separation.
Two departures diverging immediately after takeoff.
A same-course DME arc separation case.

items.explanation: FIG 6-1-3 is titled for arrival minima when landing behind an arriving aircraft requiring wake turbulence separation.

items.task: Choose the time minimum that applies until courses diverge.
items.question_text: When successive departures will fly diverging courses immediately after takeoff, what time minimum applies until courses diverge?
items.explanation: For diverging courses immediately after takeoff, the listed minimum is 1 minute until courses diverge.

lookup_context: Successive departing aircraft on same course
Following aircraft climbs through the assigned altitude of the leading aircraft
items.task: Choose the time minimum that applies until the following aircraft passes through the leading aircraft's assigned altitude.
items.question_text: For same-course successive departures, what time minimum applies until the following aircraft passes through the assigned altitude of the leading aircraft?
items.explanation: The same-course departure rule uses a minimum of 3 minutes until the following aircraft passes through the assigned altitude of the leading aircraft.

lookup_context: Takeoff direction differs by at least 45 degrees from the reciprocal of final approach course
No appropriate fix is available at a non-approach-control airport
items.task: Choose the required lead time before the arrival is estimated at the airport.
items.instruction: Apply the departing/arriving aircraft separation minimum.
items.question_text: When takeoff direction differs by at least 45 degrees from the reciprocal of final approach course, how many minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated at the airport must the departure take off?
items.explanation: When the takeoff direction differs by at least 45 degrees from the reciprocal of final approach course, the departure takes off 3 minutes before the arrival is estimated at the airport.

lookup_context: Leading aircraft maintains a speed at least 44 knots faster than following aircraft
items.task: Choose the non-DME time minimum for same, converging, or crossing course cases.
items.instruction: Apply the longitudinal separation speed-differential rule.
items.question_text: When the leading aircraft is at least 44 knots faster and the listed same/converging/crossing-course condition is met, what time minimum applies between other aircraft?

choices.text: No time minimum if the lead aircraft is faster.

items.explanation: With a leading aircraft at least 44 knots faster, the rule allows 3 minutes between other aircraft when one of the listed conditions is met.

lookup_context: Vertical separation assigned around estimated pass time
items.task: Choose the time window for assigning vertical separation before and after estimated passing.
items.instruction: Apply the opposite-course vertical separation window.
items.question_text: For opposite-course aircraft, how many minutes before and after estimated passing must different altitudes be assigned before discontinuance criteria are met?

choices.text: From 10 minutes before until 10 minutes after they are estimated to pass.
From 5 minutes before until 5 minutes after they are estimated to pass.
From 3 minutes before until 3 minutes after they are estimated to pass.
Only until both aircraft report the same waypoint.

items.explanation: Opposite-course aircraft are assigned different altitudes from 10 minutes before until 10 minutes after they are estimated to pass, unless discontinuance criteria are met.

lookup_context: Distance from NAVAID: 35 miles or less
items.task: Choose the lateral DME arc separation minimum.
items.question_text: Between different arcs about a NAVAID at 35 miles or less from the NAVAID, what lateral DME arc separation minimum applies?
items.explanation: Between different arcs about a NAVAID at 35 miles or less from the NAVAID, the minimum is 10 miles.

lookup_context: Direct course or course change of 15 degrees or less
Via NAVAIDs or radials at FL600 and below
items.task: Choose the protected width on each side of route before expansion begins.
items.instruction: Apply protected airspace along other than established routes.
items.question_text: For a direct course or course change of 15 degrees or less via NAVAIDs or radials at FL600 and below, what initial protected width applies on each side of the route?

choices.text: 4 miles on each side to a point 51 miles from the NAVAID.
5 miles on each side to a point 57.14 miles from the NAVAID.
10 miles on each side immediately from the NAVAID.
14 miles on each side below FL180.

items.explanation: For direct courses and course changes of 15 degrees or less, protect 4 miles on each side to 51 miles from the NAVAID before expanding.

items.task: Choose the lateral separation distance beyond non-overlap.
items.question_text: For RNAV aircraft on a route 8 miles or less in width, how far beyond the point where protected airspaces cease to overlap must the aircraft be?
items.explanation: For an RNAV route 8 miles or less in width, lateral separation exists when the aircraft is beyond non-overlap by at least 4 miles.

lookup_context: Example interval: 2 minutes between successive approaches
After aircraft depart the holding/approach fix inbound, longitudinal separation is used
items.task: Interpret the figure as sequencing timed approaches using longitudinal separation.
items.instruction: Use the timed approach procedure figures.
items.question_text: In the timed-approach figure using an ILS and longitudinal separation only, what interval separates successive approaches in the depicted sequence?
items.explanation: FIG 6-7-1 depicts timed approaches using an interval of 2 minutes between successive approaches.

items.task: Find the required separation in minutes at the entry point.
items.instruction: Use the Mach Number Technique table.
items.question_text: Using TBL 8-3-1, what entry-point separation is required for a 0.03 Mach difference over 1201-1800 NM?
items.explanation: TBL 8-3-1 gives 19 minutes for a 0.03 Mach difference over 1201-1800 NM.

items.task: Choose the maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.
items.question_text: In TBL 8-7-1, what maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval supports 20 NM minima with RNP 4 and RCP/RSP 240/180?
items.explanation: The 20 NM / RNP 4 / RCP 240 / RSP 180 row uses a 192-second maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.

items.task: Choose the maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.
items.question_text: In TBL 8-8-1, what maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval supports 50 NM minima with RNP 10 and RCP/RSP 240/180?
items.explanation: The 50 NM / RNP 10 / RCP 240 / RSP 180 row uses a 27-minute maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.

items.task: Choose the maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.
items.question_text: In TBL 8-9-1, what maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval supports 30 NM minima with RNP 4 and RCP/RSP 240/180?
items.explanation: The 30 NM / RNP 4 / RCP 240 / RSP 180 row uses a 10-minute maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.

lookup_context: ADS-C longitudinal separation in Anchorage Oceanic/Continental CTAs
items.task: Choose the maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.
items.question_text: In TBL 8-10-1, what maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval supports 50 NM minima with RNP 4 and RCP/RSP 240/180?
items.explanation: The 50 NM / RNP 4 / RCP 240 / RSP 180 row uses a 32-minute maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval.

lookup_context: ERAM ACL or DL Free Text Area entry
items.task: Use the clearance abbreviations table to decode the authorized abbreviation.
items.instruction: Use the ERAM clearance abbreviation table.
items.question_text: In TBL 13-1-1, what does the ERAM clearance abbreviation CAF mean?
items.explanation: TBL 13-1-1 lists CAF as cleared as filed.

lookup_context: Domestic en route CPDLC uplink message element
items.task: Choose what the response attribute requires.
items.instruction: Use the CPDLC response attribute table.
items.question_text: In TBL 14-2-1, what does the CPDLC response attribute W/U indicate?
items.explanation: TBL 14-2-1 defines W/U as response required.

lookup_context: Valid responses include WILCO, UNABLE, and STANDBY
items.task: Choose what the response attribute requires.
items.instruction: Use the oceanic CPDLC response attribute table.
items.question_text: In TBL 14-3-1, what does the oceanic CPDLC response attribute W/U indicate?
items.explanation: TBL 14-3-1 defines W/U as response required.
